claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

This will help me assess your situation properly, as Child Benefit eligibility is determined on a per-child basis.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm inquiring about my child Alex, who is 14 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's helpful to know you have a child named Alex who is 14 years old.

Now, to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, or do you have a different arrangement for looking after them?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. That's an important factor.

Now I need to check a few more details about your circumstances:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not living in the UK at the moment. I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's important information.

Since you're living abroad, I need to ask:

**Do you have Crown servant status, or are you living in a country where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit?**

(Crown servants are people employed by the UK government abroad, such as diplomats or military personnel. There are also certain countries where Child Benefit eligibility can continue even if you live abroad.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have Crown servant status or anything like that. I'm just living abroad as a regular resident.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to make an assessment.

Let me explain the situation:

You are living abroad as a regular resident without Crown servant status. According to the UK Child Benefit rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. Since you don't have Crown servant status and are simply living abroad as a regular resident, you would not typically qualify for Child Benefit under the standard rules.

The rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you **live in the UK**. While Alex is under 16 and you are responsible for them, the requirement to live in the UK is not met in your case.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time, as you do not meet the residency requirement of living in the UK.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you move back to the UK—you would be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex as long as they remain under 16 and you continue to be responsible for them.

If you have any questions or if your situation changes, feel free to reach out, or you can contact HMRC directly for further guidance on your specific circumstances.

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old and lives with the claimant, which normally satisfies the responsibility and living arrangement criteria for Child Benefit. However, the claimant is currently living abroad as a regular resident without Crown servant status. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify if you are responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant does not meet the UK residency requirement. While there are exceptions for people living in certain countries or Crown servants, the claimant has confirmed they have neither of these statuses. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit. Although they are responsible for their 14-year-old child Alex who lives with them, they do not meet the fundamental requirement of living in the UK. As a regular resident living abroad without Crown servant status, they fall outside the scope of Child Benefit eligibility according to UK government rules."

Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

As a regular resident living abroad without Crown servant status, they fall outside the scope of Child Benefit eligibility according to UK government rules."
